title: Man gets life sentence in 2024 killing of Boston father of 2
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Widmaer Lapaix, 29, from North Attleboro, received a life sentence for killing Maceo Martin, 41, in Boston in 2024.
Lapaix was convicted of second-degree murder after a nine-day trial and is eligible for parole after 15 years.
Prosecutors said Lapaix shot Martin during a domestic dispute outside an apartment in Cambridge where Martin's ex-wife lived.

title: Lindsay Clancy will admit to killing her children if trial is split into 2 parts, defense says
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's defense seeks a two-part trial where she would admit to killing her three children.
Judge previously denied two-part trial request; defense argues focus should be on Clancy's mental state.
Clancy claims auditory hallucinations before killings; trial set for July 20 in Plymouth Superior Court.

title: Patriots trade Marte Mapu to Houston Texans
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots trade Marte Mapu to Houston Texans for a 2027 sixth-round draft pick.
Mapu, initially drafted in 2023, played 17 games for New England, mainly on special teams.
Trade frees up a roster spot and creates $1.5 million in cap space for the Patriots.

title: Suspicious person prompts lockdown, canceled classes at R.I. college
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: CCRI's Warwick campus went into lockdown after reports of a suspicious man possibly carrying a weapon.
Warwick police used K-9 units and drones, finding a backpack with an airsoft gun and black pellet rifle.
Classes at CCRI were canceled for the day; police have not yet located the suspect but know his identity.

title: Red Sox get more bad news after Triston Casas suffers setback in his recovery
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Triston Casas' recovery from injuries has been stalled by a new rib muscle strain setback.
Red Sox manager Alex Cora reports Casas will undergo imaging after his hitting attempt failed.
Casas has struggled with injuries since 2024, missing significant game time after a standout 2023 season.

title: What to know about the ‘massive’ military bunker beneath Trump’s ballroom
author: New York Times Service
bullets: Trump plans a massive underground military complex beneath a $400 million ballroom project at the White House.
The underground facility will include bomb shelters, medical facilities, and secure communication systems.
A judge halted the project citing a lack of congressional approval, despite Secret Service security concerns.

title: Just one Mass. college ranks among the nation’s best graduate schools, per U.S. News
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: MIT ranked No. 1 for best graduate engineering program in 2026 U.S. News rankings.
Harvard Business School tied for fourth in the latest U.S. News graduate rankings.
Massachusetts nursing programs saw a general decline, with Regis College dropping to 99th place.

title: Suffolk DA Kevin Hayden appears to have a new primary challenger. She used to work in his office.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Linda Champion, former member of DA Kevin Hayden's team, files paperwork for Suffolk District Attorney primary challenge.
Champion, seeking to compete against incumbent Hayden, must collect 1,000 signatures by April 28 to qualify for the ballot.
Hayden faces scrutiny over charging a Boston officer with manslaughter, amidst accusations of political motivations.

title: Malden cook goes viral for selling trendy Ilocos empanadas out of her apartment
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Janz Van's Ilocos empanadas went viral but were shut down by Malden's Board of Health due to zoning laws.
Van is raising funds for a food truck and seeking pop-up partnerships to continue selling her popular empanadas.
Her empanadas attracted interest from customers as far as Canada, with high demand exceeding her production capacity.

title: Giannis Antetokounmpo paid high praise to Celtics’ coach Joe Mazzulla
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo praised Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla for his mentality and leadership during Boston's successful season.
Despite a challenging season for the Bucks, Antetokounmpo stressed his desire to maintain a winning culture in Milwaukee.
Celtics continue their strong performance without Jayson Tatum, currently holding second in the Eastern Conference.
